title: P&O Cruises South America Cruises 2026
intro: P&O Cruises brings British elegance to South America's most captivating coastlines, combining traditional cruising values with immersive exploration. From the dramatic fjords of Chile to the vibrant rhythms of Rio de Janeiro, these sailings offer authentic encounters with one of the world's most diverse continents. The line's adult-focused Arcadia and Aurora ships provide an intimate atmosphere perfect for savouring Buenos Aires' tango halls, Montevideo's colonial architecture, and the pristine wilderness of Patagonia. Onboard, passengers enjoy refined dining, enrichment lectures on South American culture, and the relaxed sophistication that defines P&O's approach. Extended port stays and overnight calls in key cities allow deeper discovery, while the British pound-based pricing offers excellent value for European travellers seeking comprehensive South American experiences without the complexity of land-based touring.

faqs.question: How much does a P&O Cruises South America cruise cost?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises South America voyages typically range from €1,299 to €3,500 per person for 14-21 night itineraries. Inside cabins start around €1,299 during shoulder season, rising to €1,599 in peak December-February months. Balcony cabins, highly recommended for scenic Patagonian and fjord cruising, cost €1,799-€2,199. Suites command €2,899-€3,499. World voyage segments offering South America portions occasionally appear at lower per-night rates. British pound pricing means favourable exchange rates can reduce costs for eurozone travellers. Flights from UK departure points add approximately €400-€800 return.

faqs.question: Which P&O Cruises ships sail to South America?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises deploys its adult-only ships Arcadia and Aurora on South America itineraries. Arcadia, carrying 2,096 passengers, offers the more contemporary experience with extensive spa facilities, speciality dining, and panoramic forward-facing public rooms ideal for scenic cruising. Aurora, slightly smaller at 1,870 passengers, provides traditional cruising with a wrap-around promenade deck and more intimate atmosphere. Both ships feature British design sensibilities, sterling-denominated onboard pricing, and dedicated enrichment programmes. Neither carries the line's family facilities, ensuring adult-focused exploration. Arcadia typically handles the longer 21-night circumnavigation routes, while Aurora concentrates on 14-18 night sector voyages.

faqs.question: When is best time to cruise South America?
faqs.answer: The optimal South America cruising season runs November through March, coinciding with southern hemisphere summer. December to February offers warmest temperatures, longest daylight hours, and optimal conditions for Patagonian glaciers and Falkland Islands wildlife. Rio de Janeiro's summer energy and potential Carnival timing (February/March) create unforgettable experiences. November and March shoulder months provide excellent value with mild weather and fewer crowds. Avoid April-October when Antarctic-facing routes become inhospitable, though northern Brazil cruises remain viable year-round. Consider that Chilean fjord navigation requires stable weather best found January-February. El Niño patterns occasionally affect precipitation; monitor forecasts when booking.

faqs.question: What is included?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises South America voyages include comprehensive full-board dining across main restaurants, buffet venues, and classic afternoon tea service. Entertainment encompasses nightly theatre productions, live music, comedy, and destination-focused enrichment lectures. Fitness facilities, swimming pools, deck games, and daytime activities carry no surcharge. Port taxes and basic gratuities are incorporated, though additional tipping remains discretionary. Aurora includes children's club programming for family sailings. Notable exclusions comprise speciality restaurants, alcoholic beverages, shore excursions, spa services, WiFi, and flights. The line's positioning as premium rather than luxury means transparent pricing without resort fees, though budget-conscious travellers should anticipate €400-€800 additional spend for drinks and excursions on a two-week voyage.

faqs.question: Which ports?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises South America itineraries encompass iconic Atlantic and Pacific coast destinations. Brazilian calls typically include Salvador with its colonial Pelourinho district, and Rio de Janeiro for Copacabana, Christ the Redeemer, and vibrant street life. Uruguay's Montevideo offers walkable Old Town and beach culture, while Argentina delivers Buenos Aires with tango traditions, Puerto Madryn for Welsh heritage and whale watching, and Ushuaia as the world's southernmost city. Pacific routes feature Peru's Lima for culinary exploration and Chile's Valparaiso, Arica, and dramatic fjord country. Falkland Islands' Port Stanley provides unique British-sub-Antarctic character with accessible penguin colonies. Tender operations at Stanley and some fjord anchorages require mobility consideration.

faqs.question: How far ahead to book?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises recommends booking South America voyages 9-18 months in advance for optimal results. World voyage segments, including South America portions, release approximately 18 months prior to departure and sell balcony and suite categories quickly. Early booking discounts typically apply 12+ months ahead, offering 10-15% savings plus cabin selection advantages. Shoulder season November and March departures may retain availability 6-9 months out, though prime December-February sailings require earlier commitment. Flight-inclusive packages from UK regional airports warrant particular advance planning for better routing and pricing. Last-minute deals occasionally emerge 8-12 weeks before departure for flexible travellers, though cabin location and dining preferences may compromise.

faqs.question: Good for families?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises South America offerings present mixed suitability for families. Aurora carries dedicated children's facilities including kids' clubs, teen spaces, and family-friendly entertainment, making 14-night voyages manageable for younger travellers. Arcadia operates as adults-only, excluding families entirely. The destination itself demands consideration: lengthy sea days between South American ports, complex historical and cultural shore excursions, and late evening dining schedules challenge younger children. Teenagers may appreciate Rio's energy and adventure activities in Patagonia. Families prioritising South America should evaluate whether P&O's British-style formality matches expectations, or whether competitors offering dedicated family programming and shorter duration options better serve multigenerational groups seeking this destination.

faqs.question: Cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: P&O Cruises applies a tiered cancellation structure increasing penalties closer to departure. Cancellations 91+ days before sailing incur deposit loss only, typically €200-€500 per person depending on voyage length and cabin category. 90-57 days prior forfeits 25% of total fare; 56-29 days triggers 50% penalty; 28-15 days demands 75%; and 14 days or less results in 100% forfeiture. Travel insurance with cancellation coverage is strongly recommended given substantial advance payment requirements and potential for itinerary disruption. COVID-19 policies evolved to offer flexible rebooking options during certain periods, though standard terms now apply. Named storm or mechanical cancellation entitles passengers to future cruise credits or full refunds at P&O's discretion, not automatic cash reimbursement.
